data.title: Wingstop rallies after same-store sales don't fall as much as anticipated
data.description:
Wingstop (WING) rallied in early trading on Wednesday after the restaurant company reported fourth-quarter earnings results.

System-wide sales increased 9.3% to $1.3 billion. Domestic same-store sales fell 5.8% during the quarter at company-owned stores, although that was better than the consensus estimate for a decline of 6.8%.

Cost of sales as a percentage of company-owned restaurant sales decreased to 75.6% from 77.6% in the prior fourth quarter. The decrease was primarily driven by sales leverage on other operating expenses, as well as a decline in food, beverage, and packaging costs primarily resulting from a decrease in the cost of bone-in chicken wings as compared to the prior fourth quarter.

Wingstop (WING) churned up adjusted EBITDA of $61.9M during the quarter vs. $56.5M a year ago.

As of December 27, there were 3,056 Wingstop restaurants system-wide. This included 2,586 restaurants in the United States, of which 2,529 were franchised restaurants and 57 were company-owned, and 470 franchised restaurants were in international markets, including U.S. territories. During the fourth quarter of 2025, there were 124 net system-wide Wingstop restaurant openings.

CEO update: "In a year marked by uncertainty, the structural advantages of our operating model are reflected in our 15% adjusted EBITDA growth in 2025. This year's performance reflects the compelling returns of our unit economics, but also the confidence in our strategy that will enable our vision of reaching more than 10,000 restaurants globally."

Looking ahead, Wingstop (WING) sees flat to low-single-digit domestic same-store sales growth. A global unit growth rate of 15% to 16% is anticipated.

Shares of Wingstop (WING) rallied 13.7% in premarket trading to $286.25 vs. the 52-week range of $204.00 to $388.14. Short interest on WING stands at 13.2% of the total float.

data.title: Moody's Q4 earnings beat as investors services revenue hits record
data.description:
Moody’s (MCO) stock rose 2.4% in Wednesday premarket trading after the company posted a hefty Q4 earnings beat and issued strong 2026 earnings guidance, as its investors services business turned in its best revenue growth for Q4 on record.

The ratings and market analytics company expects full-year 2026 adjusted EPS of $16.40-$17.00 (midpoint $16.70 vs. $16.45 consensus). Revenue is expected to increase at the high single-digit range compared with 9% growth in 2025. The company projects free cash flow of $2.8B-$3.0B vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.99B.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $3.64, topping the average analyst estimate of $3.44, decreased from $3.92 in Q3 and increased from $2.62 in Q4 2024.

Q4 revenue of $1.89B, beating the $1.87B consensus, fell from $2.01B in the prior quarter and rose from $1.67B in the year-ago period. 

Analytics revenue of $943M climbed from $909M in Q3 and $863M in the prior year’s Q4. The division’s 9% Y/Y growth includes 12% growth in Decision Solutions, 6% in Research and Insights, and 8% in Data & Information. Recurring revenue, comprising 97% of Moody's Analytics revenue, rose 11% on a reported basis and 8% on an organic constant currency basis.

Investors Service revenue of $946M compared with $1.10B in the previous quarter and $809B a year ago. The 17% Y/Y growth marks its strongest Q4 on record. Investment-grade revenue surged 97% as issuance soared 127%; high-yield bonds revenue grew 9% Y/Y, and public, project, and infrastructure finance revenue climbed 30% as issuance increased 46%. Leveraged loans revenue declined 14% Y/Y, reflecting the record issuance levels of Q4 2024.

Expenses of $1.12B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.10B, increased from $1.09B in Q3 and $1.11B in Q4 2024.

Adjusted operating margin of 48.7% slipped from 52.9% in the prior quarter and increased from 43.8% a year ago.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

data.title: Wellgistics Health invests in mental health AI startup to expand software push
data.description:
• Wellgistics Health (WGRX) said on Wednesday it has completed a strategic investment in a San Francisco-based mental health artificial intelligence startup, as it seeks to expand into the mental health software market.

  • The investment builds on the company’s partnership with Tollo Health and supports its collaboration with NFL Alumni Health, the company said.

  • Wellgistics said it is increasing its focus on patient engagement to improve prescription drug compliance and support patients initiating GLP-1 drug treatments, including through digital tools integrated with its pharmacy network.
  • The company cited industry data projecting growth in the mental health software market from $6.3 billion in 2025 to $18.1 billion by 2033.
  • WGRX +4.81% premarket to $0.27.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Workday slides as Citizens downgrades on AI worries
data.description:
• Workday (WDAY) shares slide 1.3% in premarket trading on Wednesday after investment firm Citizens downgraded the back office software company, citing continued worries about artificial intelligence. 
  • “[Aneel] Bhusri may need some time and investments to get the Workday product engine humming so it can drive the type of organic AI innovation that Workday needs to successfully transition from the SaaS era to the AI era,” Citizens analyst Patrick Walravens wrote in a note to clients. Walravens downgraded Workday to Market Perform from Market Outperform.
  • Workday announced earlier this month that Bhusri, who co-founded the company, would be returning to the CEO job, taking over for Carl Eschenbach.
  • The company is set to report quarterly results after the close of trading on Feb. 24. A consensus of analysts expects the company to earn $2.33 per share on $2.52B in revenue.

data.title: Exco Technologies gets TSX approval for share buyback
data.description:
• Exco Technologies (TSX: XTC) on Wednesday said that the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved its normal course issuer bid to repurchase up to 1,706,558 common shares.
  • The buyback, running from Feb. 20, 2026, to February 19, 2027, represents 10% of the company’s public float of 17,065,583 shares as of February 8, 2026, Exco said.
  • As of February 8, 2026, the company had 37,891,331 common shares outstanding.
  • Under TSX rules, Exco can repurchase up to 4,931 shares per trading day, representing 25% of its average daily trading volume of 19,726 shares over the past six months.
  • The company may also make one block purchase per calendar week in accordance with exchange policies.
  • Exco said it will fund the purchases through available cash and/or bank facilities.
  • EXCOF closed -0.0% at $5.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: QuickLogic awarded $13 mln contract for radiation-hardened FPGA program
data.description:
• QuickLogic Corporation (QUIK) said on Wednesday it has been awarded a $13 million contract to support development of its Strategic Radiation Hardened high-reliability field programmable gate array technology.
  • The award represents the latest tranche of a multi-year program launched in August 2022, the company said.
  • QuickLogic said the funding will support continued development and demonstration of the technology, aimed at meeting current and future strategic and space system requirements for the Department of War.
  • QUIK +3.22% premarket to $7.69.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hershey's faces backlash from the family who invented the Reese's Peanut Butter Cup
data.description:
Hershey's (HSY) iconic Reese’s Peanut Butter Cup is at the center of a controversy after Brad Reese, grandson of Reese’s Peanut Butter Cup inventor H.B. Reese, publicly accused the company of quietly degrading the candy’s core recipe and misleading consumers about what they’re buying. 

In an open letter posted on LinkedIn, Reese argued that Hershey's (HSY) brand storytelling still leans on the classic "milk chocolate + peanut butter" formula, while he maintains that modern Reese’s products no longer deliver that simple combination.

Brad Reese claims that across multiple Reese’s items, Hershey (HSY) has replaced real milk chocolate with cheaper compound coatings and true peanut butter with “peanut-butter-style crèmes,” effectively diluting the original formula that built the brand’s reputation. 

"How does The Hershey Company continue to position REESE’S as its flagship brand, a symbol of trust, quality, and leadership, while quietly replacing the very ingredients (Milk Chocolate + Peanut Butter) that built REESE'S trust in the first place?," asked Reese.

Hershey (HSY) has pushed back on the criticism, denying that it has made drastic changes to the Reese’s formula and emphasizing that any recipe tweaks are tied to expanding the product line into new shapes, sizes, and seasonal or novelty items. 

Shares of Hershey (HSY) were down 0.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday after slipping 0.8% on Tuesday.

data.title: Verde Clean Fuels to target 50% cut in operating expense
data.description:
Verde Clean Fuels (VGAS) +4.7% pre-market Wednesday after unveiling a plan to cut costs while evaluating strategic alternatives.

The company said it is targeting a 50% reduction in operating costs in 2026 compared to 2025 and has lowered director cash compensation by 80%; current directors Martijn Dekker and Dail St. Claire will not be standing for re-election at the end of their term.

Verde (VGAS) said it will shift focus to "capital-lite" opportunities to deploy its STG+ technology, including licensing technology and providing engineering, technical, and operational services, after previously deploying STG+ through development of capital-intensive commercial production plants.

The company said it has eliminated roles related to the development of capital-intensive commercial production plants that are no longer aligned with current plans.

data.title: Wingstop Non-GAAP EPS of $1.00 beats by $0.17, revenue of $175.7M misses by $1.66M
data.description:
• Wingstop press release (WING): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.00 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $175.7M (+8.6% Y/Y) misses by $1.66M.
  • System-wide sales of $1.3 billion increased 9.3% vs. 2024
  • 124 net new openings
  • Domestic restaurant AUV of $2.0 million
  • Domestic same store sales decreased 5.8% vs. 2024
  • Digital sales represented 73.2% of system-wide sales

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

    • Flat to low-single digit domestic same store sales growth;
    • Global unit growth rate of 15% to 16%;
    • SG&A of between $151 - $154 million, which includes $3 million of restructuring charges related to corporate realignment;
    • Stock-based compensation expense of approximately $32 million;
    • Interest expense, net of approximately $43 million; and
    • Depreciation and amortization of approximately $30 million.

data.title: PROG Holdings reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• PROG Holdings press release (PRG): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.74 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $574.59M (-5.2% Y/Y) misses by $7.23M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA from Continuing Operations of $61.5 million.
